title: KKR & Co. Inc. (KKR) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of KKR & Co. Inc. (KKR) compiled entirely by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 12% growth rate anticipates strong ongoing capital formation in alternative markets, driving robust fee-related earnings, counterbalanced by cyclicality in performance fee realizations.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate reflects KKR's strong market position and predictable base fee streams, adjusted for the inherent macroeconomic sensitivity of its performance-based earnings and private portfolio valuations.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate aligns with long-term global economic expansion, acknowledging the massive scale KKR operates at, which inherently restricts perpetual hyper-growth.

thesisParagraphs: KKR has evolved from a pure leveraged buyout shop into a highly diversified alternative asset management powerhouse. The structural shift of capital moving from public to private markets represents a massive, multi-decade tailwind. Institutional investors continue to allocate heavily towards private equity, infrastructure, and real estate in search of yield and uncorrelated returns.

While the current market price implies robust future growth expectations, potentially limiting the margin of safety, the underlying business fundamentals are incredibly strong. KKR's ability to gather 'sticky,' long-dated capital provides highly predictable, recurring fee-related earnings. The expansion into private wealth and retail channels presents a massive untapped opportunity for future AUM growth.
verdictFooter: Based on financial actuals and Gemini estimates. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 12% growth rate for KKR?
faq.answer: Gemini projects strong continued inflows into alternative assets, driving high-margin fee-related earnings, while factoring in the lumpy nature of performance fees dependent on exit environments.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for KKR's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.0% discount rate was selected, reflecting the stability of locked-up capital fees balanced against the higher risk profile of private equity valuations in uncertain economic conditions.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice.
